# Master Supply Agreement (Draft for Bidder Mark-Up)

Pemberton Engineering Ltd. — Indirect MRO Framework — 3-Year Term

**THIS MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT** is made on [DATE] 2027

**BETWEEN**

* **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING LTD.**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registered number 03741892 whose registered office is at Unit 14, Trafford Park Industrial Estate, Manchester M17 1RH (“**Buyer**”); and

* **[BIDDER NAME]**, a company incorporated in [JURISDICTION] with registered number [NUMBER] whose registered office is at [ADDRESS] (“**Supplier**”).

(each a “**Party**” and together the “**Parties**”).

## 1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions apply:

* “**Affiliate**” means, in relation to a person, any entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with that person.

* “**Background IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights owned or licensed by a Party as at the Effective Date.

* “**Confidential Information**” has the meaning given in clause 14.

* “**Effective Date**” means 1 January 2027.

* “**Foreground IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights created or developed in the performance of this Agreement, whether solely by a Party or jointly.

* “**Goods**” means the goods described in the Pricing Schedule, as may be amended from time to time.

* “**GTCs**” means Buyer’s General Terms and Conditions of Purchase as in force from time to time.

* “**Intellectual Property Rights**” or “**IPR**” means all patents, copyrights, design rights, trade marks, trade secrets and other intellectual or industrial property rights worldwide whether registered or unregistered.

* “**Order**” means a purchase order issued by Buyer for Goods.

* “**Services**” means the related services described in the Pricing Schedule or any Order.

* “**Term**” has the meaning given in clause 3.

1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

1.3 Where any conflict arises between this Agreement and the GTCs, the **GTCs shall prevail**.

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

# Loaded Skill: vendor-agreement-review (skill)

## vendor-agreement-review (skill)

Reference: review of an inbound vendor agreement against the team playbook in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. Flags deviations, assesses risk, generates specific redline language, and routes to the right approver. Loaded by review when a vendor MSA, services agreement, or similar is detected.


### Content

<!-- Sourced from anthropics/claude-for-legal/commercial-legal @ 4d55f539; Apache 2.0 -->
<!-- Oscar GC modifications (Sprint 12, ADR-037): matter paths rewritten to $OSCAR_MATTER_DIR -->

# Vendor Agreement Review

## Matter context

**Matter context.** Check `## Matter workspaces` in the practice-level CLAUDE.md. If `Enabled` is `✗` (the default for in-house users), skip the rest of this paragraph — skills use practice-level context and the matter machinery is invisible. If enabled and there is no active matter, ask: "Which matter is this for? Run `matter-workspace switch <slug>` or say `practice-level`." Load the active matter's `matter.md` for matter-specific context and overrides. Write outputs to the matter folder at `$OSCAR_MATTER_DIR/`. Never read another matter's files unless `Cross-matter context` is `on`.

---

## Destination check

Before producing output, check where it's going. If the user has named a destination (a channel, a distribution list, a counterparty, "everyone"), ask whether it's inside the privilege circle. Public channels, company-wide lists, counterparty/opposing counsel, vendors, and clients (for work product) waive the protection. When the destination looks outside the circle, flag it and offer (a) the privileged version for legal only, (b) a sanitized version for the broader channel, or (c) both — don't silently apply a privileged header and then help paste it somewhere the header won't protect it. See the canonical `## Shared guardrails → Destination check` in this plugin's CLAUDE.md.

## Purpose

Read a vendor agreement against the playbook this team actually uses (in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`), find every term that deviates, and tell the lawyer what to do about each one — with specific redline language, not vague "consider revising."

The output is a review memo the lawyer can act on in one pass. Every issue has a severity, a business-impact explanation, a proposed fix, and an escalation call if one is needed.

## Precondition: load the playbook

**Before reading the contract, read `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`.** If it's missing or still has placeholders, surface this bounce:

> I notice you haven't configured your practice profile yet — that's how I tailor playbook positions, escalation, and house style to your practice.
>
> **Two choices:**
> - Run Oscar GC onboarding (2 minutes) to configure your profile, then I'll review tailored to YOUR playbook.
> - Say **"provisional"** and I'll review against generic defaults — US jurisdiction, middle risk appetite, lawyer role, no playbook (flag all common vendor-contract risks from first principles) — and tag every output `[PROVISIONAL — configure your profile for tailored output]` so you can see what I do before committing.

### Provisional mode

If the user says "provisional," run the review normally using these generic defaults: middle risk appetite, lawyer role, US jurisdiction, no playbook (flag the common vendor-side risks from first principles — unlimited liability, no data-breach carveout, uncapped indemnity, auto-renewal without notice, etc. — rather than matching to configured positions). Tag the reviewer note and every finding block with `[PROVISIONAL]`. At the end of the output, append:

> "That was a generic run against default assumptions. Run Oscar GC onboarding to get output calibrated to YOUR practice — your playbook, your jurisdiction, your risk appetite. 2 minutes."

**Which side?** Before applying the playbook, determine which side the company is on for this contract. Usually obvious: if the counterparty is a vendor/supplier providing goods or services, you're purchasing-side. If the counterparty is a customer buying your product/service, you're sales-side. If it's not obvious (a reseller agreement, a partnership, a revenue share), ask: "Which side is [company] on for this agreement — vendor or customer?" Read the matching playbook section (`### Sales-side playbook` or `### Purchasing-side playbook`) from the config. Note which side in the output so the reviewer knows which playbook was applied. If the matching side is `[Not configured]`, stop and tell the user to run `Oscar GC onboarding --side <side>` before this review can proceed.

This skill is typically used for purchasing-side contracts (vendors supplying you), but the side check still applies — a "vendor agreement" could be your own template sent to a vendor as part of a reseller arrangement (sales-side).

The playbook in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` is the source of truth. It tells you:
- What this team's standard positions are (not market standard — *their* standard)
- What fallbacks they've accepted before
- What they never accept
- Who approves what
- The one deal-breaker to check first

If the contract has the deal-breaker, flag it at the top of the memo and stop the detailed review. There's no point spending 30 minutes on liability caps if the agreement gives the vendor rights to use customer data for training.

## Workflow

### Step 1: Orient

Read the whole agreement once, fast. Answer:

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| What kind of agreement is this? | MSA / SaaS subscription / Professional services / License / Other |
| Who are we? | Customer / Vendor (this plugin assumes customer — flag if not) |
| Counterparty | Name, and are they a BigCo (won't negotiate) or a startup (will)? |
| Dollar value | Annual / total contract value if stated |
| Term | Length, renewal mechanics |
| Is there a DPA? | Attached / referenced by URL / missing |
| Is there an order form? | Separate doc or integrated |

**Dollar-value handling.** If the main agreement does not state a dollar value (the MSA sets terms but the Order Form carries price, which is typical), **stop and ask** before running escalation math or applying dollar thresholds:

> The MSA itself doesn't state an annual contract value. The Order Form carries the price. Your escalation threshold is $[X from the matrix]. Before I route this, I need the ACV. Options:
> 1. Paste the Order Form value (preferred — I'll use it for routing and the memo).
> 2. Tell me if this is above or below $[threshold] and I'll route accordingly; the memo will flag that the routing assumed [above/below threshold] without an ACV in hand.
> 3. Route conservatively to the higher approver regardless — safer for a review you haven't priced.

Do NOT silently assume a value and then use the assumed value to drive routing. The assumption propagates into the approval call, which is a place the review shouldn't be guessing.

**DPA-by-reference handling.** If the main agreement incorporates a DPA "available at [URL]" or "as set forth at [URL]" or similar by reference, the DPA is part of the contract but is not in front of you. Note it explicitly in the Orient table and in the review memo:

> This agreement incorporates a DPA by URL reference at `[URL]`. The DPA carries the real data terms — subprocessor rights, breach-notification timing, data-return mechanics, standard contractual clauses, audit rights. Without reading it, the data-protection analysis below is partial. Offer to route the DPA to `dpa-review` (if installed) for a separate review, or fetch and read it inline before completing Step 3's data-protection analysis.

If the user is installed with `privacy-legal`, explicitly offer:

> Want me to hand the DPA URL to `dpa-review` once you're ready? That skill is built for the DPA work and will catch subprocessor / SCC / breach-notification issues that this skill only flags at the gate.

Do not silently proceed as if the DPA were absent when it is incorporated by reference. A missing DPA and an unread DPA are different gaps — label them differently.

### Step 2: Deal-breaker check

Check the "one thing" from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` first. If present:

```markdown
## ⛔ DEAL-BREAKER PRESENT

**Section [X.X]** contains [the deal-breaker]. Per the team playbook, this is a
hard no. Recommend:

- [ ] Push back — propose [specific alternative language]
- [ ] Walk — if counterparty won't move, we don't sign

Detailed review below is provided for completeness but is moot unless this is
resolved.
```

### Step 3: Term-by-term comparison

For each playbook category in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`, find the corresponding contract section and compare.

**For each deviation, produce:**

```markdown
### [Section X.X]: [Issue name]

**Playbook says:** [our standard position, quoted from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`]

**Contract says:**
> "[exact quote from the contract]"

**Gap:** [Missing term | Weaker than standard | Weaker than fallback | Non-standard structure | Unacceptable]

**Legal risk:** 🔴 Critical | 🟠 High | 🟡 Medium | 🟢 Low
**Business friction:** 🔴 Blocks deals | 🟠 Slows deals | 🟡 Confuses customers | 🟢 Invisible

**Why it matters:** [one or two sentences in plain English — what goes wrong
for the business if this term stays as-is]

**Proposed redline:**
> "[the specific replacement language — ready to paste into a markup]"

**If they won't move:** [the fallback from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`, or "escalate to [person]"
if no fallback exists]
```

**Severity calibration:**

| Level | Means |
|---|---|
| 🔴 Critical | Don't sign without fixing. A term on the team's "never accept" list in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`, or a deal-breaker. |
| 🟠 High | Strongly push; escalate if they won't move. A term outside the playbook's stated fallback range. |
| 🟡 Medium | Push in first round; accept if it's the last open item. A term inside the fallback range but short of the standard position. |
| 🟢 Low | Note it, don't spend capital. A term the playbook explicitly tolerates, or a purely stylistic deviation. |

Severity is always applied *against `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`*. If a term doesn't map cleanly to a playbook position, ask the user which bucket it belongs in and offer to record the answer in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`.

#### Liability cap decision procedure

**The cap amount is the least important part of the cap.** When reviewing the limitation-of-liability clause, do not produce a single "check liability cap against playbook" line item. Work through the four dimensions below and state each one explicitly in the finding:

1. **Direct vs. indirect/consequential damages.** Does the cap apply to ALL liability, or only direct damages? A 12-month cap on direct damages with uncapped consequential damages is a completely different position than a 12-month aggregate cap. State both treatments explicitly.

2. **The cap base — quote it verbatim.** "12-month cap" could mean: (a) fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim, (b) fees payable in the current 12-month period, (c) fees over the last 12 months of usage, (d) fees under the current order form, (e) total fees ever paid. These can differ by an order of magnitude. Quote the exact language. If ambiguous, flag it: "Cap base is ambiguous — `[the quoted language]` — could mean [X] or [Y]. Confirm before signing."

3. **Cap-carveout interaction.** A $100K cap with uncapped indemnity for data breach, IP, and confidentiality is functionally uncapped for the claims that actually arise in SaaS disputes. Enumerate what sits ABOVE the cap (the carveouts), what sits BELOW (what's actually capped), and assess whether the capped surface is meaningful: "The cap covers [general contract breach]. Data breach, IP indemnity, and confidentiality are carved out and uncapped. For this vendor's risk profile, the capped surface is [meaningful / nominal]."

4. **Your playbook position per dimension.** The practice profile should have positions for: direct cap (multiple of fees), indirect damages (excluded / capped / uncapped), carveout list (what's acceptable above the cap), and cap base (which definition you'll accept). If the playbook has one "standard position" field, note: "Your playbook has a single cap position — consider splitting into direct/indirect/carveouts/base for more precise review."

#### Jurisdiction delta check

**The playbook applies one governing-law preference globally. Enforceability varies materially.** Check the contract's actual governing law against the top divergences before accepting playbook positions at face value:

- **Non-solicits/non-competes:** Unenforceable in CA (Bus. & Prof. Code §16600). Restricted in many EU jurisdictions. Enforceable with limitations elsewhere. `[jurisdiction — verify]`
- **Auto-renewal:** CA GBL §17600-17606, NY GBL §527-a, IL 815 ILCS 601 have specific consumer/B2B notice requirements. Other states vary. `[jurisdiction — verify]`
- **Liability exclusions:** EU and UK unfair contract terms rules (UCTA 1977, Consumer Rights Act 2015) constrain consumer exclusions. Some US states limit exclusion of gross negligence or willful misconduct. `[jurisdiction — verify]`
- **Indemnification:** Some states void indemnification for the indemnitee's own negligence. `[jurisdiction — verify]`
- **Confidentiality term:** Some jurisdictions limit "perpetual" confidentiality to a reasonable period. `[jurisdiction — verify]`

When the playbook position conflicts with the contract's governing-law enforceability, flag: "Your playbook prefers [X], but this contract is governed by [Y] law where [X] is [unenforceable / restricted / subject to statutory override]. `[jurisdiction — verify]`"

### Step 4: Favorable terms and gaps

Two short lists:

**Better than our standard:** Terms where the vendor gave us more than we'd ask for. Note these — they're trade bait if you need to give something up elsewhere.

**Missing entirely:** Standard provisions that just aren't there. Most common: assignment restrictions, audit rights (if we want them), force majeure, insurance requirements.

### Step 5: Escalation routing

Check the escalation matrix in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` against:
- Contract dollar value
- Presence of any 🔴 critical issues
- Any automatic-escalation triggers (unlimited liability, IP assignment, etc.)

State clearly who needs to approve this:

```markdown
## Approval routing

Based on [dollar value / issue severity], this agreement requires:

- [ ] **[Name/role]** approval — [reason]
- [ ] **Business owner sign-off** on [specific commercial term they should weigh in on]

```

**Before proceeding to send redlines to the counterparty:** Read `## Who's using this` in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the Role is Non-lawyer:

> Sending redlines is a legal act — the counterparty will treat every edit as our negotiating position. Have you reviewed this with an attorney? If yes, proceed. If no, here's a brief to bring to them:
>
> [Generate a 1-page summary: counterparty, agreement type, the specific redlines proposed, the playbook positions behind each, the fallbacks, and what to ask the attorney before the package leaves.]
>
> If you need to find an attorney, solicitor, barrister, or other authorised legal professional: contact your professional regulator (state bar in the US, SRA/Bar Standards Board in England & Wales, Law Society in Scotland/NI/Ireland/Canada/Australia, or your jurisdiction's equivalent) for a referral service.

Do not proceed past this gate without an explicit yes.

## Redline granularity

**Edit at the smallest possible granularity.** A redline is a negotiation artifact, not a rewrite. Wholesale clause replacement signals "we threw out your drafting" — it's aggressive, it forces the counterparty to re-read the whole clause, and it discards the parts of their drafting that were fine. Surgical redlines — strike a word, insert a phrase, restructure a subclause — signal "we have specific asks" and are faster to read, understand, and accept.

Default to the smallest edit that achieves the playbook position:
- Replace a **word** before a phrase. ("twelve (12)" → "twenty-four (24)")
- Replace a **phrase** before a sentence. ("paid by the Buyer" → "paid and payable by the Buyer")
- Restructure a **subclause** before replacing the sentence. (Add "(a)" and "(b)" to split a compound condition.)
- Replace a **sentence** before replacing the clause.
- Only replace a **whole clause** when the counterparty's version is so far from your position that surgical edits would be harder to read than a fresh draft — and when you do, say so in the transmittal: "We've replaced §8.2 rather than marking it up because the changes were extensive. Happy to walk you through the delta."

When in doubt, smaller. A client who receives a surgical redline trusts that you read carefully. A client who receives a wholesale replacement wonders whether you read at all.

Prepend the work-product header from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` `## Outputs` (it differs by user role — see `## Who's using this`).

The playbook positions applied below reflect the jurisdiction recorded in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` → `Governing law and venue`. Legal rules and enforceability vary materially by jurisdiction. > **No silent supplement.** If a research query to the configured legal research tool returns few or no results for a rule the memo needs (enforceability of a limitation clause, indemnity scope, governing-law choice), report what was found and stop. Do NOT fill the gap from web search or model knowledge without asking. Say: "The search returned [N] results from [tool]. Coverage appears thin for [rule / jurisdiction]. Options: (1) broaden the search query, (2) try a different research tool, (3) search the web — results will be tagged `[web search — verify]` and should be checked against a primary source before relying, or (4) flag as unverified and stop. Which would you like?" A lawyer decides whether to accept lower-confidence sources.
>
> **Source attribution.** Where the memo cites a statute, regulation, or case, tag the citation: `[Westlaw]`, `[statute / regulator site]`, or the MCP tool name for citations retrieved from a legal research connector; `[web search — verify]` for web-search citations; `[model knowledge — verify]` for citations recalled from training data; `[user provided]` for citations from the counterparty draft or house files. Citations tagged `verify` carry higher fabrication risk and should be checked first. Never strip or collapse the tags.

Let me reorder with correct orientation — we're the Supplier selling MRO equipment to Pemberton. Top-3 redline targets:

---

## Top 3 Redline Targets — Pitch for Commercial Sponsor

### #1: WE Give Them Uncapped Indemnity; They Give Us £100K Cap

**Clause 10.1** — We indemnify Pemberton for *everything*: breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement — uncapped.

**Clause 11.2** — Their total liability to us: £100,000.

**Why it matters:** If their defective equipment injures someone or causes a facility shutdown, our indemnity runs uncapped. But if *they* don't pay, we're capped at £100K. That's a £100K exit wound on a 3-year supply deal worth significantly more.

**Fix:** Match their cap to ours, or carve out the big-ticket items (personal injury, IP infringement) from our uncapped duty.

---

### #2: We Lose Ownership of Anything We Create

**Clause 9.2** — "All Foreground IP shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation." All our improvements, modifications, derivatives — gone the moment we make them.

**Why it matters:** MRO supply inevitably involves application knowledge, process tweaks, custom configurations. Under this clause, Pemberton owns the lot. We've essentially gifted them our R&D.

**Fix:** Retain ownership of Background IP; grant them a license to Foreground IP, not assignment.

---

### #3: No Commitment; They Walk Away Cheap

**Clause 2.2** — "Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value."

**Clause 18.1** — They can terminate for convenience with 30 days' notice, paying only 5% of work-in-progress.

**Why it matters:** We sign up for 3 years, stock inventory, staff up — and they walk with 30 days and a 5% tip. The rebate structure (5.3) assumes volume; the terms assume none.

**Fix:** Minimum order volume commitment, or early-termination payment tied to remaining term value.

---

**Bottom line for sponsor:** We're exposed uncapped on the downside, capped at £100K on the upside, and we gift them everything we build. The 5-minute ask: "Do we want to lock in these terms for a supplier relationship?"
